item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It ended up without any sort of explanation to the entity shown with last picture.

Maybe Point out that the doctor got affected by this dream state and saw the picture while looking for poor D-class?
Maybe point out that one of the faces shown behind the woman is face of the unfortunate D-class?
Maybe add some more cases in which the woman ate their target?

This has so much potential currently not used. And only the hope for its potential development is keeping me from down vote.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree with that. The first two logs almost make for what would otherwise be a really bland article, but the other two kind of kill the story being told just to put a couple of creepy images without telling nothing whatsoever.

I'm leaning to downvote right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to agree with what has already been said; this has really great potential but as it is it leaves me wanting. The build up is nice, the freaky lady with the morphing mouth is creepy and the concept is really clicking with me. But then it just quits at the end, with no real payoff from the build up. It needs more, of what I'm not sure I can articulate. But it has the potential to be great, it just ends too soon.

Keep going with it, I like what you have so far!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for researcher not being a dick towards D-class. Actually apologizing and making his life LESS awful… wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you might wanna do that sometime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are two images that said "Click to enlarge." However, if click the images, i only see the message that there are no file existing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I could upvote on potential, I'd give +2. This has incredible potential, but as others have said, it stops right when it's primed to become super interesting. The possibilities for further exploration logs are literally endless. Go to another state! Country! Go into space!

The head that eats people just seems like a convenient way to end the exploration, rather than a plot point leading further on. Same with the final image. It just looks like a 'GAME OVER' screen, and doesn't provide enough context to even question who she is, what she represents or how she's related to this dream world.

This has so much room to grow, but right now it seems like an unfinished draft, so no vote. I really hope you expand on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty sure that means you're dreaming right now

wake up

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even though others say that this article gets increasingly weirder and that ruins it, I honestly love it in its current state.
It just makes you wonder what is exactly going on, and even though the final image is admittedly comical, it left chills when I first saw it.
+1 from me.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
"the last picture seems to be from some chinese photo gallery site. i asked somebody to look through the terms and services and they said everything in the site has to be public domain, so there shouldn't be any problems, right?"

Does this mean a bucket load of fuel for SCP writers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really disappointed, because it was really good to start with, and the monster reveal was really cool, then it just more or less ground to a halt. The method of transportation by… eating…? was kind of silly, and the final scene offered not much to advance my interest. Overall, a -, but I am hopeful that after the event you'll be able to refine this into a truly good SCP.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: You are correct about ivanpah solar power facility, I misread that. As you've sourced the rest to my satisfaction and removed the non-compliant image, this is resolved. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dream SCPs are usually nice and scary, and this is no exception. Well done for showing that you don't need an eldritch horror to do a good scary SCP.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
i can get rid of the sleep study room picture but the ivanpah solar power facility picture doesn't seem to contain any non-commercial clauses? im not too familiar with creative commons so please correct me if im wrong.

also i did some digging and found the sources for the last two pictures:

9. East oblique of missile site control building - Stanley R. Mickelsen Safeguard Complex, Missile Site Control Building, Northeast of Tactical Road; southeast of Tactical Road South, Nekoma, Cavalier County, ND by Halpern, Benjamin

untitled by 鳲来

the last picture seems to be from some chinese photo gallery site. i asked somebody to look through the terms and services and they said everything in the site has to be public domain, so there shouldn't be any problems, right?

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Ivanpah Solar Power Facility and Sleep Study Room are non-compliant with the site license as they contain non-commercial clauses. The visual records site is not itself sufficient sourcing. You need to find the original posting of the images and they need to be compliant with the site license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really see why this wasn't crosslinked to the existing oneironautics, but rather appears to be a parallel approach to the same concepts. I could see this as the origin of the Foundation onieronautics program, actually.

Otherwise, I liked this well enough.

I don't get the title though. Is it a reference to something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You are currently viewing this warning while inside a dream.

Enjoyed that.

This worked really, really well right up until the woman's head (which was incredibly creepy at first) ate the D-Class and then he couldn't wake up anymore. The rest felt a bit comical, especially the final image. That's particularly a shame, because this felt like 093 in a really good way up until then. I was excited to find what else was gonna come up after the woman's head thing… and then she just ate him, and it turned out she was the 'final' element as opposed to one step into a larger, freakier world.

Hope to see this taken in another direction — I'd love to see where else it could go.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
oop

here are the images used in this article, in order of appearance:

Ivanpah Solar Power Facility by Craig Dietrich

FuBK wide by wikipedia user "Ughhhg"

Sleep Study Room by Chiara Ogan

field countryside country wheat by pixabay user "Foundry"

the last two pictures are from the visual records wiki:

pyramid thingy is under the "architecture" section
mask lady is under the "humanoid" section

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeah im definitely going to expand this later

i had a few ways i wanted to end this but i started writing only a few days before the posting period ended so i just went with the most straight forward way to end it. judging from the feedback im getting, this was not a smart idea, so i'm going to come back to this after the contest ends and rework it so it has a less shitty ending

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't do a whole lot for me. There's a lot of good work in the setup (the dream warning was neat, the operator/subject relationship was cool, the sharp break with them losing contact and then reconnecting was quite cool). Then there's things that initially were interesting but in retrospect of not being paid off are just loose ends (why a solar farm, were those index cards really there IRL, how does the operator know exactly when to say things are going to get weird and we just don't happen to read anything before that?). The female figure was definitely the weakest part, mouth-conforming and all. Just this motiveless but threatening weird figure.

It's definitely, like others have said, very much "Setup setup setup setup AND THEN THINGS GOT VERY BRIEFLY WEIRD, WOAAAAAAAAHHHHH, SO WEIRD, the end."

If you're trying to use that last image to play into the what is reality/who is dreaming kind of stuff…. it didn't work.

-1 I guess. This could definitely use some development.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit I loved this concept and I loved the execution. Upvoted and bookmarked. Maybe more exploration logs would be better, you carried my attention throughout the whole skip really well

item.title: Good Job
item.content:encoded: Whether this wins the contest or not does not matter. If you polish just a few things up, then this is a very solid SCP. I love the story, and I personally think it is the best read i have had in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm split between agreeing with this and liking the ambiguity of the ending. In either case, the ending does feel really rushed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. This doesn't do much for me.

Your set up is solid, but as Cyan and shaggy have pointed out you cut things off right when they really start to draw the reader in, leaving me more frustrated than intrigued. If you get a chance to when the contest ends, I'd consider expanding on this. As is, however, it just kind of spins its wheels without going anywhere.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The research outpost looks like a solar power tower. Is it supposed to be a solar power tower? Like, is there some reason that a solar power tower is the best place to study this anomaly?

Subject was placed in an intensive care unit. It is not currently known if recovery is possible.

Wouldn't the subject have already been medically set up so that he could stay asleep for at least several days, in which case him just not waking up wouldn't need any change? Or did something happen to the subject beyond just no waking up, like having a seizure or showing bodily damage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see how 106 is even remotely comparable to this.

Seconding Cyan in that this ended far too suddenly. It cuts off right when it was getting juicy. I don't feel like there's enough context to really appreciate whatever is implied by the final log.

ETA: 'Implued' is my new favorite word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dream skips have a high bar to clear as far as I'm concerned, and this one doesn't.

Further testing has revealed that post-wake memory retention is reduced when an individual is affected by SCP-3000. The average estimated degree of recall for all subjects involved in SCP-3000 testing has been measured to be less than 10%.

Okay, so how do they know it exists? How did they figure out this was in any way anomalous in the first place? The whole "we use anomalies to magically record dream footage" is somehow even more disbelief-breaking. I just can't reconcile that at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's because I was always fascinated with dreams since a young age, but I really like his one. Captivating and creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started out interesting, but it kind of feels like 106 but a lady and she only attacks dreaming people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy, yes, but a little cheesy near the end. That was an abrupt end without much explanation that felt very tonally different than the body of the article, in a bad way. Maybe I'm missing something, but I don't understand the significance of the female figure, or why that's the end. Is it insinuating that the reader is dreaming? Because I thought the opening warnings confirmed that the reader isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whew, creepy. I'm very skittish, so I refuse to click the "Click to enlarge" images.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: alright, i changed it to a static image

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a huge fan of the gif at the start of the article. It gave me a headache and was too distracting for me to really read much past the description. If it wasn't a gif, it wouldn't be as much of a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i wrote this in two days

this was the worst idea of my life but i hope you enjoy it anyway

+ attribution
- attribution

FuBK wide by wikipedia user "Ughhhg"

field countryside country wheat by pixabay user "Foundry"

9. East oblique of missile site control building - Stanley R. Mickelsen Safeguard Complex, Missile Site Control Building, Northeast of Tactical Road; southeast of Tactical Road South, Nekoma, Cavalier County, ND by Halpern, Benjamin

untitled by 鳲来
